HMW-V02: Job Detail Section Ordering

How might we order the sections on the vendor's job detail page so the most important information for their workflow (fee reconciliation vs operational context) appears first?
Context: The vendor job detail page has 5 sections below the Status Action Bar: Route, Cargo, Fees, Proof of Service, Activity Log. The current design preview puts Route + Cargo first (top-left), Proofs + Activity (top-right), Fees full-width below. But the vendor app's primary purpose is fee reconciliation. The question: should fees be front-and-center, or should operational context (route, cargo) come first because the vendor needs to understand the job before reviewing fees?

Option A: Operations First (Route → Cargo → Fees → Proofs → Log)

Chronological
Follow the natural workflow: vendor sees what the job IS (route, cargo), then what it COSTS (fees), then what's been DONE (proofs, log). This mirrors how the admin slide-out works — route context first, then fees below. The vendor reads top-to-bottom like reading a job sheet.
Pros
• Natural reading order — understand the job before reviewing numbers
• Matches admin slide-out layout (platform consistency)
• Route/cargo context helps vendor spot fee errors ("wait, this was only 12 bags, not 24")
Cons
• Fees are below the fold — vendor has to scroll to reach the primary purpose of the app
• Route/cargo rarely changes — vendor already knows where the job is (they did it)
• Prioritizes information the vendor already has over information they need to check
← My Jobs J01 Export Customs Clearance DO-2603-001 · TikTok Global · MAWB 160-12345678
In Progress Upload proof of service to mark complete
Section order (top → bottom)
① Route
Origin
Baoan WH, Shenzhen
29 Mar · 08:00
→
Destination
SZX Cargo Terminal
29 Mar · 16:00
② Cargo
Bags
24
Weight
1,280 kg
Volume
8.4 CBM
③ Fee Breakdown ↓ (below the fold)
Vendor must scroll down to see fees — the primary reconciliation data
④ Proof of Service
⑤ Activity Log

Option B: Fees First (Fees → Route + Cargo → Proofs → Log)

Recommended
Lead with the money. The vendor opens a job detail for one of two reasons: (1) take action (handled by Status Action Bar at the top), or (2) check the fees. Put the fee breakdown immediately after the action bar. Route and cargo are reference info — the vendor already knows the route because they executed it. Fees are the thing they need to verify.
Pros
• Primary purpose (reconciliation) is above the fold
• Vendor sees fees without scrolling — faster workflow
• Cargo quantities sit right below fees, easy to cross-reference ("24 bags × CNY 15 = 360, correct")
• Signals that this app is about reconciliation, not just job tracking
Cons
• Breaks from admin slide-out ordering (route first there)
• New vendor on first login might not understand the job context without seeing route first
• Fee table is the densest section — leading with density could feel intimidating
← My Jobs J01 Export Customs Clearance DO-2603-001 · TikTok Global · MAWB 160-12345678

SZX Cargo Terminal
29 Mar · 16:00
③ Cargo
Bags
24
Weight
1,280 kg
Volume

④ Proof of Service
2 documents uploaded · Upload zone below
⑤ Activity Log
4 entries · Reverse chronological

Option C: Split View (Fees Left, Context Right)

Balanced
Side-by-side layout on desktop: Fees as a persistent left column, Route + Cargo + Proofs + Log scrollable on the right. Both priorities visible simultaneously without scrolling. At 768px, stacks to fees-first (same as Option B).
Pros
• Both fees and route visible at the same time on desktop
• Vendor can cross-reference fees against cargo quantities without scrolling
• Feels like a reconciliation workspace, not just a detail page
Cons
• Fee table is constrained to ~50% width — tight for 5 columns
• More complex layout to build and maintain
• Collapses to single-column at 768px anyway, so the benefit is desktop-only
• Different layout pattern from the rest of the app
← My Jobs J01 Export Customs Clearance DO-2603-001 · TikTok Global · MAWB 160-12345678

Baoan WH, Shenzhen → SZX Cargo Terminal

Cargo
Bags
24
Weight
1,280 kg
Vol
8.4 CBM
Proof of Service
2 documents · Upload zone
Activity Log
4 entries

Recommendation: Option B — Fees First

The vendor app exists for reconciliation. Lead with the money. The vendor already knows the route (they executed the job). What they need to verify is: are the fees correct, are the quantities right, which fees did Teleport remove? Put that at the top.

Option A is the "safe" admin-consistent ordering, but it optimizes for understanding a job you haven't seen before — that's the ops planner's workflow, not the vendor's. Option C is elegant on desktop but adds layout complexity for a benefit that disappears at 768px (where it collapses to fees-first anyway).

Option B is simple, opinionated, and directly aligned with the app's primary purpose. Status Action Bar → Fees → Route + Cargo (side by side) → Proofs → Activity Log.
